episode_title: AI work tools are getting easier to use, not just smarter
selected_story_title: OpenAI is trying to make AI feel more useful at work
selection_reason: This is the clearest lead story for the edition because it directly matches the headline and the practical takeaway: AI is moving toward everyday work tasks, not just bigger demos or louder claims.
first_spoken_line: Today’s story is not about an AI model becoming more dazzling for its own sake. It is about a change that matters much more for normal people: OpenAI says its new GPT-5.6 family is aimed at getting more done with less effort, especially in coding, writing, and knowledge work.
final_spoken_line: So the bottom line is simple. This is not just a story about a smarter AI. It is a story about a more usable AI. If the tool helps you get from rough idea to usable draft with less friction, that is real progress. If it only shifts the work around, then the promise is still ahead of the practice.
